driver_principal: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire and its implications on Middle East geopolitical stability and global energy markets are the dominant forces shaping current market dynamics.
hechos_confirmados: A tentative two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is in place, with a face-to-face meeting scheduled in Pakistan for April 10-11, 2026.
Israel has continued bombing targets in Lebanon, causing significant casualties and jeopardizing the broader Middle East ceasefire.
Iran maintains control over the Strait of Hormuz and is pushing forward with plans to charge a toll on vessels passing through it.
Oil prices have risen above $100 per barrel due to concerns over restricted energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict.
escenario_dominante: There is a moderate to high probability that the ceasefire between the US and Iran will hold in the immediate term, supported by ongoing diplomatic efforts and scheduled talks. However, localized conflicts, such as Israeli strikes in Lebanon and tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, will likely persist, maintaining elevated risk premiums on energy markets and contributing to sustained inflationary pressures globally.
interpretacion_institucional: The market is cautiously optimistic about the ceasefire holding, as reflected in recent equity rallies, but skepticism remains due to ongoing violations and regional tensions. The inability of Treasuries to rally signals concerns about persistent inflationary effects from the conflict. Control of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran introduces a structural risk premium on oil prices, likely sustaining elevated energy costs and influencing central bank policy stances. Investors should monitor diplomatic developments closely, as well as inflation data, to gauge the durability of the détente and its macroeconomic impact.
riesgos_secundarios: Escalada militar en el sur de Líbano debido a los ataques israelíes contra Hezbollah, que no están cubiertos por el alto el fuego.
Persistencia o intensificación del bloqueo o control iraní sobre el estrecho de Hormuz, afectando el flujo global de petróleo.
Fracaso o retraso en las negociaciones diplomáticas entre Estados Unidos e Irán en Pakistán.
Reacción negativa de los mercados financieros ante sorpresas inflacionarias mayores a las esperadas en los datos de CPI y PCE.
Posible aumento de tensiones geopolíticas derivadas de movimientos militares o sanciones adicionales en la región.
eventos_clave: Reunión entre funcionarios de Estados Unidos e Irán en Pakistán el 10-11 de abril de 2026.
Publicación del índice de precios al consumidor (CPI) de Estados Unidos para marzo el 10 de abril de 2026.
Publicación del índice de gastos de consumo personal (PCE) de Estados Unidos para febrero el 9 de abril de 2026.
Evolución de los ataques israelíes en Líbano y su impacto en la estabilidad del alto el fuego.
Decisiones y comunicados de bancos centrales, especialmente la Reserva Federal, en respuesta a datos inflacionarios y riesgos geopolíticos.
